reviews.review_description:
If you enjoy spending your free time doing industrial maintenance and playing "Space Electrician," then this is the game for you. For everyone else? Stay far away.
I went into this expecting a sci-fi adventure, but instead, I got stuck in a never-ending loop of tedious, "Paint dry " puzzles. You spend 90% of the time walking at a snail's pace through dark rooms just to find a battery pack or flip a switch to open a door. It’s not "atmospheric"—it’s just plain pathetic and slow.
There is zero action for the longest time. If you’re looking to actually use your controllers for fighting or killing anything, forget about it. It’s just chore after chore. The graphics look great on the Quest 3, but beautiful scenery doesn't make up for a game that feels like a shift at a factory.
Save your money and buy something that actually has some adrenaline. This is easily one of the most boring experiences in VR

If you find games like this tedious and boring, that's understandable. But not all of us want to play games where the main objective is to kill everything in sight. To each their own, but if that's the entire premise of your 1-star review, then you're likely hating the genre, not the game itself IMO. 

That being said, I'm about halfway through this game and what I love about it (besides the amazing graphics and overall visuals, not to mention how precise the controllers and hand movements are) is how solvable the puzzles are. They are challenging enough to make you think and work for it, but not so much that I wanted to rage quit or cheat and look up the answers. I found the pace and storyline perfect for my preferences.

Another win for the devs, keep them coming please 🙏😀

I played Red Matter and loved it. It was short, but awesome! Then I got this one and continued the game. WOW! I am so impressed by the graphics and gameplay! The work that went into this is so impressive! I thoroughly enjoyed this one and was upset when it finally ended. The puzzles are challenging but not so hard that you need a whole lot of help. I didn’t need any, but I love puzzle games and will stick to it until I figure it out. You are guided enough that you can solve every one of them and go where you have to. I absolutely LOVED this! I really hope they continue with a Red Matter 3! I will be one of the first to buy it. Bravo to the creators! Thanks for a wonderful experience! 😊

I give it a 5 because its incredibly detailed, immersive, and beautiful environments more than overcame its average story and puzzles. Right out of the gate, I was stunned while looking at Saturn’s rings. The tunnels/passageways were all the same, but they were often interrupting by unique, interesting, and artistic/spooky scenarios. One part of the game, in which you search for the fate of the crew, had a dark, detailed, and ominous vibe that made me feel fully invested in learning more. I think that the pistol/blaster/thing overall detracted from the game. Shooting the droids was fun and could compliment the game during stressful times. However, it often ended up interrupting the story instead. It also didn’t help that the pistol seemed to shoot everywhere except where you aim it.

I’ve played quite a few puzzle games, and this one is one of the best for puzzles and adventure. There are so many great things about this game. The graphics are amazing, and the game feels long compared to others, so you don’t feel like you blew through the money you spent too quickly. It can be challenging at times but never feels too hard or frustrating.

I love how intricate everything is—the amount of detail in the environments, puzzles, and world design makes it incredibly immersive. The action level feels balanced and works really well with the adventure aspect of the game. I haven’t even finished it yet, and it already has some of the best graphics I’ve ever seen in VR.

​When playing standalone on Quest, I am someone who values optimization—how stable and smooth the game runs—far more than eye candy like how pretty the graphics look. However, the graphics of Red Matter 2 made me realize the importance of visuals to some extent. Throughout my playthrough, I felt that this level of visual quality was worth sacrificing a bit of performance for.
​To be honest, the optimization of this game isn't perfect. In environments with a lot of dynamic lighting, the frame rate easily drops below 72. I felt it was a bit regrettable and wished they had limited the dynamic lighting and shadows.
​But one thing is clear: the graphics are better than Red Matter 1, yet the optimization is actually much better, which was very satisfying. I played with the background music completely turned off, and the immersion of being all alone in the dead silence of space felt highly realistic and great. ​However, due to the nature of this type of gameplay, the replay value isn't very high. Despite the overall fantastic and wonderful experience, I don't think I will play through it a second time.

​My device is the Meta Quest 3. Meta’s ongoing OS updates have made the system significantly heavier than before, and I believe this is degrading standalone game performance. Therefore, the performance baseline you used during your initial testing is likely no longer the same as what players experience today.

developer_response.body: Hey there, could you tell us on what device you've been playing? It should run at 72fps at all time. Is it Quest 2 by any chance? Please feel free to contact us at info@verticalrobot.com

reviews.review_description: This game really displayed to me what vr can do. The graphics, atmosphere, music, dialogue, puzzles and gun play all rounded one of the best vr experiences ive ever witnessed... id go further and say one of the best games ive played on any platform. An absolute must for any gaming enthusiast!

Well...once I figured out how to use the jet pack, I was away. I played the first red matter and really enjoyed it but this one blew me away. the graphics are amazing especially the grand scale of some of the scenery. there is far more interaction, granted some of the tasks took some figuring out....( I cheated a bit and had a look at the walk through on you tube...SHHHH don't tell anyone ) I'm absolutely loving it and not even halfway through... just got the gun, blew my toes of a couple of times but hey ho! if your looking for a shoot 'em up arcade game this isn't for you.. the task with the laser terminals are piggin difficult but worth it when you crack them. can't wait for the next chapter. All in all I would recommend this game and definitely  worth every penny ( plus I got it on offer lol! ) Enjoy

Okay, if after having played Half Life Alyx you thought that there is nothing left for VR, you'd be right--until you've experienced Red Matter 2, that is.
As everyone has said, graphics are on par with PC; matching and in some cases beating HLA... The HLA fan will spot the occaisonal HLA heist (in several scenes a red slime covers the walls, nearly exactly as rendered in HLA).
Fourtunately the content creators did not! go down the boring ol' Lone Echo 2 route and provide Red Matter 2 with similar levels of tedium and boredom, because with Red Matter 2 we get guns! well, a gun, anyway, and enough baddies firing back at you to induce some adrenaline to your vascular system. Thank you, thank you devs for not going down the tired tedium of Lone Echo 2. Thank you for the guns and subsequent excitement. The combat is no doubt that which separates Red Matter 2's nearly-perfect 4.9 rating from Red Matter 1's rather lackluster 4.5 rating. The combat and perhaps the jet pack. My compaints? except for the fact that the game has an ending, not much... There were several times Sasha got stuck in a crevice and could not get freed no matter what I did (the "please crouch" error message, which is dev speak for "your character is stuck and we haven figured out how to free him..."). The only solution was total reboot of the game.
Also, the gun could use a bit more accuracy.
We all eagerly  wait for Episode 3 of Red Matter...

reviews.review_title: Decent sequel with some new ideas
reviews.review_description:
Good, all be it an abrupt end.  Honestly I kind of like the more puzzle-centric focus of the first Red Matter game even if the light gunplay was a nice all be it eventually repetitive addition.  

Both titles are obvious showcases of pushing the quest headsets to their limit for the time.  Definitely worth picking up one on sale though regardless. 

Clearly there's enough lore hanging with the games namesake red matter for a third title if they wanted.  Though they more or less wrapped up the storyline well enough with some unknowns to keep people wondering, which more often than not actually leaves for a better story in my opinion but might annoy some.

good
the art style amazing great graphics on the quest loved it, communist brutal-ism style everywhere, I loved the solar panel puzzle, the moon surface aesthetic looked nice with the train rings like a music album cover. allot of puzzles I liked also the one where you had to get a battery by climbing an elevator with a chair and gas can to get the battery to the previous floor. I like the idea of the ending you can't have what you want if I can't have what I want revenge like.

bad
some of the puzzles just feels unfair or strange, like the locker the first time you see the ai eye robot, to the last one in the office room is crazy hard it was not clear even the astronaut was a strange one to get part of a code, that one was easy in that office you need 4 the globe was normal but the eye and shelf code where a bit out there. allot of walk to the next puzzle.

I am a hard one to please so 4 stars

Played this after Red Matter. This game will surely test your fear of heights. Graphics were amazing. Story line was fun to follow. Loved the combination of puzzle and shooter/survival sequences. 

Hopefully there is a Red Matter 3!

This game was so awesome and I absolutely loved it. Only thing I have to say about the game is that there are some puzzles that I struggled on because you had to look very carefully for the pieces needed. Had to use a walkthrough on some parts lol

Easiest 5 stars I have ever given anything, I wish I could give it more.  Seeing Saturn for the first time up close in VR is still the #1 experience I have ever had in VR.  Thanks again for this game!!!
This game is really realistic and detailed, and is really fun to be able to go to other planets, would do 10 stars if it was an option.

Don't often leave reviews but I really enjoyed playing this game. The graphics were amazing on my Quest 2. There was a good story line and the puzzles were just right, not too easy. Little bit of action too. Really recommend anyone considering buying this game.
Stunning graphics, mysterious story, wonderful sound design. Gameplay is a combination of easy and challenging puzzles as well as the original but with the addition of fight mechanics.

Just finished playing Redmatter 2 having owned quest 3 for two weeks. Enjoyable, great visuals, interesting puzzles and some basic combat. It can be played at your own pace standing or sitting. I am retired so sitting while playing and occasionally standing to experience full vr environment. Lot of time spent looking around or through windows. If my room IRL was bigger I could walk around within the game more. Attention to detail superb with lots in house scfi joke memes. Pity there is not more games like this on the quest. Might encourage me to try escape rooms because of interaction within environment.

There is no better game on this platform, amazing graphics, amazing music, direction, in short 10/10. It surpasses all the games released so far and even those already released by several chapters. This game alone is worth buying Meta Quest 3!

From amazing  graphics to clever puzzles and a superb story line, this is everything I want in a game.  Add in friendly and timely customer support, and you have a winning combination that is hard to beat.  Two thumbs up!

Game is incredible.  Unreal visuals (without a pc), very immersive.  They did a great job incorporating the controllers to emulate hand motions. Just enough of a challenge to not borrow you, but easy enough to get great game flow. 5 stars.

This game hits all the notes for me. Decent amount of gun fire (I don’t think they needed to ash that element for 2 but it wasn’t overkill).  I actually am not clear on the story line… there’s lots of cards and notes to read, and much of it is about story, and not helpful to solutions. I wasn’t really there for the story.  I played these games for the puzzles, the sci-fi, and the absolutely stunning visuals.  This game ruined all other games thus far because of how well-done it is - everything else looks cheap, blocky, and unrealistic by comparison.  It’s certainly has a “WTF” element, which I like.  What else can I find that feels as immersive?  Hard to beat.

Loved Red Matter I, but II is ruined by turrets, bots, and drones.  The creepy quiet atmosphere is shattered by the shooter/combat requirement.  The game was better than I had wished for until it was made into just another shooter game. Why change the formula?

If I ever finish this game, I won't play it again. I've played the first game several times. I love everything about it, but this one leaves me anxious and uncomfortable. This is not fun once the shooter levels begin. But it is an expensive game, and it's probably too late to get a refund once you're that far into the game. I'm giving this 2 stars because the combat ruined this game and it ruined the atmosphere and fun of simply exploring the fantastic settings. I knew there was combat in this, but hoped it would be just a few instances, then I could get back to everything that makes Red Matter so exceptional, but every level , once combat begins, has more turrets, bots, and drones.  I'm about 3/4 in and don't think I can take anymore and finish.

If Red Matter 3 ever happens, I don't want it.
When I finished this game i had a feeling like what is next, some void inside me… Not a lot of game like this on the market today! I recommend to play!

Red Matter was the first game that really drew me into VR and I had to know how the story completed. And Red Matter 2 doesn’t disappoint. It has the same great storytelling ability and sucks you right into a new space other worldly experience.

I am not much of a gamer. FPS and games where you have to spend hours crafting things is not something I enjoy. Red Matter 2 (I did not play Red Matter 1) hits every checkmark for me. It is visually stunning. The details down to reflections of items are so detailed. The puzzles are fun and for about 2/3 of the game are solvable with a normal human mind. lol I needed to get help from YT. But that makes it fun for me. If I am stuck for more than an hour I will look for help and then the "ahhhhh that's how you do it" is enjoyable too. Some of the things that make it difficult is the accuracy of the gun, the jetpack takes time to get used too, and it is tedious that you have to use it to  go up a tiny step. Some of the puzzles that require you to accomplish a skill can be hard too as you have to be precise and that can get tedious. But it is fun. And I also enjoy the way you can sometimes solve a puzzle more than one way with the things round you. It is cool to look around a room like a normal person and try to figure out how you can accomplish something by using a number of things in the room. And on top of all that, it is a good story. They can do so many spin-offs with this. I am looking forward to more from these developers. (note: I did find a glitch in one part of the game where there was a battery charger in a drawer. I picked it up and dropped it on accident, and it just disappeared. So I had to restart from the previous save.)
Realy
This game is fantastic, good story, great puzzles, and amazing graphics. I loved it from the beginning to the end!

One of the best games on quest. The best graphics on any vr game I've ever played on my quest 3 . Literally shocked by how amazing this game is and confused why there isn't more games that look like this . I would spend so much money

I know why you are reading this review. The questions you are asking are probably along the lines of - should I buy or pass on this game, should I buy the first installment, is there a need to play the first game, etc. As someone who has played both back to back, I can say without a doubt that you can skip the first version. The first game is short, with dubious puzzles that required some googling, lending itself for me to deem it as being more of a demo to the second part. Red Matter 2 is the complete, polished game. Problem solving is more straightforward, graphics are even better than the first, and the campaign is much, much longer than the first. I found Red Matter 2 to be more engaging than the first one.
The best graphics ever, it’s so incredible the level of quality for standalone

So I like puzzles and quests and have every single game that has puzzles in it. This is a great, long and interesting game, it has a story, everything you do has a purpose. The puzzles are real life situations, like use a pipe to prompt the door and crawl underneath so it doesn’t feel like a quest where you try to match pictures with tubes , it feels like you’re living life and using common sense. It also has a tiny bit of shooting and turrets to keep you from boredom but it’s super  easy and it was absolutely great since I don’t like too much action.
This game is incredible from top 5 games to me

I loved Red Matter and was wondering where they were going to go next with Red Matter 2. The second installment is a much expanded game with more locations, puzzles, more action and more jeopardy. The story continues where we left off, and we set off to find a Strelka N3 survivor. 

I really enjoyed the action elements of the game with the time limited jetpack boosts and the drone attacks adding some real jeopardy. The puzzles were great as always. I also loved the continuation of the story with us finding out what happened to Irina and Stas. 

Definitely worth your time

This is a beautiful successor to the first game. 

First rate visuals, even on a Meta Quest 2. I mean, GORGEOUS environments.

Great voice acting - reminiscent of HALO/Cortana. In a sense, loses the sense of isolation from the first game; up to you if that's good or bad.

What this doesn't do well is the dang jetpack or whatever it is that let's you jump.

Controls are not intuitive.
There's no tutorial, while there is one for grabbing things.
Sometimes the pack works, and you hover. Sometimes you don't, and you just sit there.
I still don't understand how to use it correctly  and when it does what I want I can't immediately replicate the controls. 

EDIT: The developer's response doesn't help; by pressing and holding the right joystick, sometimes the pack works, sometimes it doesn't. No problem was solved by their response.

There's also no height adjustment in game, which is WILD for a VR game.

And for God's sake, give me a flashlight.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!
The height adjustments is in the settings, try turning seated mode on or off.
There certainly is a flashlight that is unlocked midway through Toska Station in the earlier chapters.
As to the jetpack, you just need to hold the right joystick up. That's it.

reviews.review_description: The first game was amazing, this one was even better. The puzzles are very simple and not too difficult all you need to do is take a moment to breathe and process. The story is wild and really enjoyable. This one isn’t as much of a horror game as the first one in terms of jumpscares but it’s very intense. I could not recommend this game enough if you enjoy puzzle games

reviews.review_title: Red Matter 2 the gold standard for VR
reviews.review_description: first the visuals are incredible the texture and detail shows of what the quest can do when done right.  much improvments over the first one. You can get lost with the storyline but the game is straight foward. The right amount of fighting to puzzle solving. IF you are thinking about it i highly recommend It as a great game.

Rarely has a game fascinated me as much as Matter 1 and 2. Many thanks to the team. The game concept, execution, and tricky but solvable puzzles were a lot of fun. All good things come in threes? Perhaps there will be a sequel.

reviews.review_title: A Beautiful but Sometimes Confusing Game
reviews.review_description:
Red Matter 2 improved on physically EVERYTHING from the original. The story was easier to understand, the gameplay was less repetitive and the puzzles were much less frustrating. However, I will say that some of the puzzles were definitely annoying and instead of making me feel proud of solving them, I just felt a sense of “finally, I can move on.”
Example: in the O2 refill room, once you kill the robots and make your way down, you find a battery in the back of a radio by looking on the back. Smart. But when I power the elevator, I need another battery to power the door. So I try to open the electric heater thinking it was the same (it was not). Turns out you have to use the grabbers to pull the battery off the elevator and back towards . Took me 30 minutes to figure out as I play standing and I grab everything manually to add to the fun. I wouldn’t have figured this out had I not randomly found it while walking through for the fifth time. 
There were a few other puzzles like this, but other than that I had tons of fun beating it. I hope that if they continue the series (whether under another character or maybe as a prequel), they try to make less puzzles depending on mechanics that are entirely optional or unused to that point, especially considering I was 2/3 done with the game then. Otherwise, I loved the game and hope to see more.

The best VR game I have ever played. Every aspect of this game has been carefully crafted, the story line, the puzzles, challenges, the physics, the various sceneries. Just hours and hours of pure enjoyment and great challenges.

This game is a true VR game, breathtaking. Massive congratulations to the team that developed it. You should be proud.
If you're looking for as real as it gets this is it. I'm still looking for something that's even close
Been playing vr for years and lemme tell ya I am BLOWN away by the graphics on this one! Just beautiful and makes me almost just a little made that other games don't look this way

From a console player - i Love , love , LOVED part 1 and 2. Sci-fi, a slight horror element,  beautiful graphics , interesting story , fun and intimidating puzzles. I wish the game was longer and would've loved more secrets to find. A gem of a game. Thanks everybody who made it happen.

reviews.review_title: Red Matter 2 delivers the VR goods!
reviews.review_description: I just finished Red Matter 2 tonight. Graphically, this is one of the best looking games on the Quest 3 that I’ve come across. It’s a a single player adventure game that has players solving puzzles and moving through the linear storyline. The story is solid but not amazing. It’s a puzzle game with a decent amount of first person shooter style gun play. There some fun drones that you control as well. The puzzles are interesting and very from easy to hard enough to have me running  to YouTube for help .Where it excels is atmosphere and graphics.

I was a little skeptical at first because I own Red Matter 1 which to me was not a huge winner.  Red Matter 2 far surpasses the graphics, user experience, story line and just the depth of the puzzles.  Not too hard, and not too easy.  It rocks.

The graphics are even more stellar with this game that it's predecessor, and everything is an overall improvement. There's some light action, and I played this one immediately after the first to finish up the story. 

Once again, it's a rather convoluted story, but it is intriguing. The story doesn't do anything spectacular or special, but the graphics are excellent and the game really is eye candy in VR. The puzzles are more interesting in the sequel, and getting to change environments a few more times was a nice upgrade from RM1. The tracking works well and there are a number of puzzles and obstacles to find your way around that take advantage of this using very minute movements in constrained puzzles. 

I would recommend this game to others overall, but don't go into it thinking it's a full on action game; there is some action, but it is confined to specific sequences that fit well into the overall narrative.

I just finished the game and want to play again just to experience certain scenes — particularly the ones involving views of the planets and terrain. It’s like Brink Traveler for outer space. 

The graphics are also top notch for a stand alone app. Amazing design, music, and physics.
the best game ever
This is an amazing series and a third game would be an amazing thing if you guys could make one
Ending got me, wont spoil anything its worth the money

Played this a while ago and just bought 2. If you like escape rooms and even if you don’t, this is just an amazing environment to experience with challenging puzzles to get to the next room. Storyline is also superb.

Highly recommend playing red matter original to understand the plot of the game. This is a sci-fi mystery puzzle like game where you get to travel to bases around Saturn and Neptune. The graphics are amazing so if you love space and astronomy you will love this game
cool
Red Matter 1 & 2 completed. Waiting Red Matter 3.

The only thing is that i wouldn't pass the part where you needed to find oxygen tanks because one of mine was missing and i couldnt find it anywhere. Yet it is still a very fun and puzzling game, and It really got my mind working and the effect that your alone and need to solve mysteries makes it a thrilling and fun game! P.S. Pls make a rewind button or smth.btw only four stars because i got stuck.

developer_response.body: Hi! I think the oxygen tank issue is due to a walkthrough video where the YouTuber is picking up the wrong one. If you skip forward a few seconds you will realize he was wrong. You have no idea how may people that video has mislead haha

reviews.review_description: When we have to open the doors to toska idk how to do the beeping part

developer_response.body: Hey there! Do you mean the 3D holographic puzzle? Grab the pointer with your hand, and move it. You'll notice that there is a tiny blue arrow in the pointer, move it in the direction it's indicating. Let me know if that helps, if  you need more help feel free to email us at info@verticalrobot.com

reviews.review_title: like on a PC, except more nauseating
reviews.review_description: this is not much better than a PC game, it's nothing like the difference VR makes for racket and club sports.

The second best being metro: Awakening, and the first overall game being HLX.
The game has amazing graphics, the best you can get on standalone with smooth gameplay. It has immersive mechanics, it's fun to play and the puzzles are challenging not hard. They are difficult AND fun.
The game fixed just about everything bad from the first game. Any jankyness, annoying puzzles etcetera are gone and fixed.

reviews.review_title: The Gold Standard for Graphics on Quest
reviews.review_description: As a relatively new VR player, I am absolutely blown away. I have never encountered a game with such stunning, sharp, and breathtaking graphics. As of right now, this is easily the best-looking game on the platform. The attention to the smallest details is incredible, making it so easy to get fully immersed and lost in the world. I truly wish we had more games this well-crafted, pushing the visuals to a level that feels like true PC VR. A masterpiece!

reviews.review_title: Fantastic game well worth the money
reviews.review_description: I just finished the game and man what an experience the graphics are great and the immersion is 10/10 really hope they come out with a 3rd one

This game is absolutely amazing! The graphics are insane. The puzzles are extremely fun and make you want to not stop playing. And on top of the graphics it feels very immersive and is just beautiful. I highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: Red Laser Prop from Last Starfighter!!!
reviews.review_description: Awesome game like the last one. Clearly made by real ones because of how awesome all the sets are.

There's nothing like this game, beautiful graphics, and clever puzzles I was captivated by the story and felt part of the game and was actually sad about the ending 
Hands down the best game I've ever played on VR

If you are into puzzle games, then this is a must have. Red Matter 1 is alright, but Red Matter 2 is absolutely amazing. Great story, really cool puzzles to solve and probably the best graphics on Quest 3

I've just completed this game and really enjoyed it from the start to the finish. Absolutely fantastic game play.i am now looking for a game to match the standard of this one!!! , any recommendations would be appreciated 👍
I really like this it was so much fun and the story line was very interesting if I could give it 10 stars I would

reviews.review_title: A story full of puzzles and combat
reviews.review_description: Wow, just finished the game. Took me about 15h. This is very good. There were a few very hard puzzles (lucky find some help on YT).
I love the graphics, the gameplay, the movement, and the fact that each area is a new puzzle - and the solutions are not repetitive. This is a must play for anyone.
Simple puzzles.
Good game until about 8 hours in it turns into a shooter game with the world's worst gun.

reviews.review_title: I loved the 1st one this one not so much
reviews.review_description: The reason why is the drones. Im stuck after the train area. Where my battery dies. I keep dying even though I move really fast . Why is there no option to disable the drones or shoot them? It's annoying. I may just give up and delete the game. It's a beautiful game. In real life you could disable drones or throw something at it. This game is a bit too stealthy for me. Thanks for the quick response. I will try this. Im sorry it just frustrating. I love you games. Keep up the great work.

developer_response.body: Hi there! There is a setting in the game to make the combat difficulty easier, if you need more help please contact us at info@verticalrobot.com We would be very glad to help you out. A few tips also, keep in mind you can sprint by clicking the left joystick and holding it down as you move. Other users who have trouble prefer to use teleport here to quickly move around. And lastly I would recommend to time your movements, if you sprint whenever the turrets are looking away, you should be able to make it past pretty easily.

reviews.review_description: It's a great game 🙂

reviews.review_title: Best graphics on any vr game
reviews.review_description: This game on quest 3 is unbelievable. The graphics are absolutely mind blowing. I sometimes just stare at it all and don't even move
Fantastic game

reviews.review_title: Boring quest room in ussr thematic
reviews.review_description: The world is empty, the movement is slow. I'm not getting sick in VR but this game made me feel sick. The puzzles are not intuitive, but the bigger problem is that walking around is so slow, that I got bored with the puzzles that the game offers very quickly.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Keep in mind that you can sprint by holding down the left joystick, and if you are feeling dizzy in any way, please check the comfort settings and adjust them until you feel comfortable enough :)

reviews.review_description:
The story and campaign are so good they are challanging and that makes it so good
The end really hit me.
Must have!
Loved every moment!!!!!
10/10 Would highly reccomend

reviews.review_title: No need fight or stealth if not ready
reviews.review_description:
I stuck on mining cave dodging laser, the flying jet is laggy and stuck on ceiling cannot landing got bug, I should encourage to add diff element on game like fight shooting or stealth but should without bug.

By changing the movement way to teleport I able to pass the cave part and finish the game, but less reality experience on it, still more prefer red matter 1

developer_response.body:
Hey Jia,
we have never heard about a bug like that, but if you are stuck for some reason you can go to New Game, and select the last unlocked chapter. This will restart from the last chapter and unstuck you. If you need more help feel free to email us at info@verticalrobot.com

reviews.review_title: Outstanding and gorgeous in every way!
reviews.review_description:
I am brand new to VR, and this was the second game I purchased after getting the quest 3. I am nearly finished in the game and I cannot express how much I have thoroughly enjoyed every minute of that masterpiece! I must’ve spent half of my time, simply looking around and wowing at the graphics, clarity and textured details. So beautifully done! The puzzles, the story, the interface of the tools, the physics, all brilliantly done. ￼ I am in fact, getting slightly anxious to being so close to finish that game. What the heck am I gonna do afterwards? Pray unceasingly for Red Matter 3? Will do!  Seriously, the only negative of this game is that it has now spoiled my enjoyment for every other game or apps I have played with on my quest so far. Why don’t all the other games look as sharp and beautiful as this!? Honestly, I have been seriously disappointed by the lower quality and clarity of all the other games I’ve explored so far. :-(

This game should installed by default on the Quest3 in order to perfectly demonstrate the full potential of the Q3 hardware when developers are fully dedicated to creating the best possible content for this platform. 

Highly recommended and my deepest thanks and appreciation to the ace developers behind this VR masterpiece! Bravo! 👏👏👏
